name: 2016 Assembly of Experts Election
description:
The fifth Iranian Assembly of Experts election was held on 26 February 2016 to elect 88 members to eight-year terms. The [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council) disqualified approximately 80% of applicants — 801 registered; only 166 were approved — including every woman who applied and virtually the entire reformist slate. Among those rejected was [Hassan Khomeini](/people/hassan-khomeini), grandson of the Islamic Republic's founder, whose exclusion drew international attention and was seen as a signal of hardliner dominance over the body that selects the Supreme Leader.

The election was held concurrently with parliamentary elections. Despite the severe pre-screening, the 2016 Assembly of Experts cohort was considered somewhat more moderate than its predecessor — reflecting the political space created by the Rouhani presidency and the 2015 nuclear agreement. The [Assembly of Experts](/orgs/assembly-of-experts) elected from this cycle was notable for convening in March 2026 to select a new Supreme Leader following the death of [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei).

sources.title: 2016 Iranian Assembly of Experts election — Wikipedia
Reformists under Fire as Election Officials Reject Candidates
Elections in Iran: 2016 Majlis and Assembly of Experts Elections

editorial_notes: Held 26 February 2016 concurrently with 10th Majlis election. 801 applied; 166 approved. All women disqualified. Hassan Khomeini rejected. Reformists won approx. 20 of 88 seats.
